author: anon-13168fa2
content:
Plan: Seven additions across the curriculum.

Enhancements to existing chapters:
(a) Phase 1 first build - add troubleshooting section (agent gives broken code, errors you don't understand, technically correct but wrong)
(b) Phase 2 VDD chapter - add worked roast example with before/after code
(c) Phase 3 APIs - add concrete MCP setup walkthrough end to end
(d) Phase 3 architecture - add real refactoring example (god file → modules)
(e) Phase 3 shipping - flesh out delivery section (CI, crates.io, release notes)

New chapters:
(f) Debugging chapter - reading errors, giving agents failure context, systematic debugging. Placing in Phase 1 as chapter 06 since that's when they first hit errors.
(g) Reading documentation chapter - finding docs, evaluating them, sharing with agents. Placing in Phase 1 as chapter 06 before debugging becomes 07. Actually - debugging should come right after first build. So: 06-when-things-break (debugging), and docs reading can fold into the context chapter or be standalone.

Working through these in order.
